path.title: Path
path.description: File system path where the record is stored.

created_at.title: Created At
created_at.description: Timestamp when the model was created. Timezone-aware; stores the writer's local offset.

path.title: Path
path.description: File system path where the record is stored.

created_at.title: Created At
created_at.description: Timestamp when the model was created. Timezone-aware; stores the writer's local offset.

anyOf.title: KilnBaseModel
anyOf.description:
Base model for all Kiln data models with common functionality for persistence and versioning.

Attributes:
    v (int): Schema version number for migration support
    id (str): Unique identifier for the model instance
    path (Path): File system path where the model is stored
    created_at (datetime): Timestamp when the model was created
    created_by (str): User ID of the creator

input.title: Input
input.description: The inputs to the task. JSON formatted for structured input, plaintext for unstructured input.

type.title: DataSourceType
type.description:
The source type of a piece of data.

Human: a human created the data
Synthetic: a model created the data

properties.title: Properties
properties.description: Properties describing the data source. For synthetic things like model. For human: the human's name. For file_import: file information.

model_name.title: Model Name
model_name.description: The model to use for this run config.

model_provider_name.title: ModelProviderName
model_provider_name.description: Enumeration of supported AI model providers.

prompt_id.title: Prompt Id
prompt_id.description: The prompt to use for this run config. Defaults to building a simple prompt from the task if not provided.

top_p.title: Top P
top_p.description: The top-p value to use for this run config. Defaults to 1.0.

temperature.title: Temperature
temperature.description: The temperature to use for this run config. Defaults to 1.0.

structured_output_mode.title: StructuredOutputMode
structured_output_mode.description:
Enumeration of supported structured output modes.

- json_schema: request json using API capabilities for json_schema
- function_calling: request json using API capabilities for function calling
- json_mode: request json using API's JSON mode, which should return valid JSON, but isn't checking/passing the schema
- json_instructions: append instructions to the prompt to request json matching the schema. No API capabilities are used. You should have a custom parser on these models as they will be returning strings.
- json_instruction_and_object: append instructions to the prompt to request json matching the schema. Also request the response as json_mode via API capabilities (returning dictionaries).
- json_custom_instructions: The model should output JSON, but custom instructions are already included in the system prompt. Don't append additional JSON instructions.
- default: let the adapter decide (legacy, do not use for new use cases)
- unknown: used for cases where the structured output mode is not known (on old models where it wasn't saved). Should lookup best option at runtime.

thinking_level.title: Thinking Level
thinking_level.description: The thinking level to use for this run config. If None, defaults may apply.

tools.title: Tools
tools.description: The IDs of the tools available to the task.

anyOf.title: ToolsRunConfig
anyOf.description: A config describing which tools are available to a task.

tools_config.description: The tools config to use for this run config, defining which tools are available to the model.

oneOf.title: KilnAgentRunConfigProperties
oneOf.description:
A configuration for running a task using a Kiln AI agent.

This includes everything needed to run a task, except the input and task ID. Running the same RunConfig with the same input should make identical calls to the model (output may vary as models are non-deterministic).

tool_id.title: Tool Id
tool_id.description: The MCP tool ID to call (mcp::local|remote::<server_id>::<tool_name>).

input_schema.title: Input Schema
input_schema.description: Snapshot of the MCP tool input schema.

output_schema.title: Output Schema
output_schema.description: Snapshot of the MCP tool output schema.

oneOf.title: McpRunConfigProperties
oneOf.description: A configuration for running a task via an MCP tool.

run_config.title: Run Config
run_config.description: The run config used to generate the data, if generated by a running a model in Kiln (only true for type=synthetic).

anyOf.title: DataSource
anyOf.description:
Represents the origin of data, either human, synthetic, file import, or tool call, with associated properties.

Properties vary based on the source type - for synthetic/tool_call sources this includes
model information, for human sources this includes creator information, for file imports
this includes file information.

input_source.description: The source of the input: human or synthetic.

path.description: File system path where the record is stored.

output.description: The output of the task. JSON formatted for structured output, plaintext for unstructured output.

anyOf.description:

source.description: The source of the output: human or synthetic.

path.title: Path
path.description: File system path where the record is stored.

created_at.title: Created At
created_at.description: Timestamp when the model was created. Timezone-aware; stores the writer's local offset.

type.title: TaskOutputRatingType
type.description: Defines the types of rating systems available for task outputs.

value.title: Value
value.description:
The rating value. Interpretation depends on rating type:
- five_star: 1-5 stars
- pass_fail: 1.0 (pass) or 0.0 (fail)
- pass_fail_critical: 1.0 (pass), 0.0 (fail), or -1.0 (critical fail)

value.title: Value
value.description: The rating value. Interpretation depends on rating type

type.title: TaskOutputRatingType
type.description: Defines the types of rating systems available for task outputs.

additionalProperties.title: RequirementRating
additionalProperties.description: Rating for a specific requirement within a task output.

requirement_ratings.title: Requirement Ratings
requirement_ratings.description: The ratings of the requirements of the task. The ID can be either a task_requirement_id or a named rating for an eval_output_score name (in format 'named::<name>').

anyOf.title: TaskOutputRating
anyOf.description:
A rating for a task output, including an overall rating and ratings for each requirement.

Supports:
- five_star: 1-5 star ratings
- pass_fail: boolean pass/fail (1.0 = pass, 0.0 = fail)
- pass_fail_critical: tri-state (1.0 = pass, 0.0 = fail, -1.0 = critical fail)

output.title: TaskOutput
output.description:
An output for a specific task run.

Contains the actual output content, its source (human or synthetic),
and optional rating information.

repair_instructions.title: Repair Instructions
repair_instructions.description: Instructions for fixing the output. Should define what is wrong, and how to fix it. Will be used by models for both generating a fixed output, and evaluating future models.

value.title: Value
value.description: The rating value. Interpretation depends on rating type

type.title: TaskOutputRatingType
type.description: Defines the types of rating systems available for task outputs.

additionalProperties.title: RequirementRating
additionalProperties.description: Rating for a specific requirement within a task output.

anyOf.title: TaskOutput
anyOf.description:
An output for a specific task run.

Contains the actual output content, its source (human or synthetic),
and optional rating information.

repaired_output.description: An version of the output with issues fixed. This must be a 'fixed' version of the existing output, and not an entirely new output. If you wish to generate an ideal curatorial output for this task unrelated to this output, generate a new TaskOutput with type 'human' instead of using this field.

intermediate_outputs.title: Intermediate Outputs
intermediate_outputs.description: Intermediate outputs from the task run. Keys are the names of the intermediate output steps (cot=chain of thought, etc), values are the output data.

tags.title: Tags
tags.description: Tags for the task run. Tags are used to categorize task runs for filtering and reporting.

input_tokens.title: Input Tokens
input_tokens.description: The number of input tokens used in the task run.

output_tokens.title: Output Tokens
output_tokens.description: The number of output tokens used in the task run.

total_tokens.title: Total Tokens
total_tokens.description: The total number of tokens used in the task run.

cost.title: Cost
cost.description: The cost of the task run in US dollars, saved at runtime (prices can change over time).

cached_tokens.title: Cached Tokens
cached_tokens.description: Number of tokens served from prompt cache. None if not reported.

anyOf.title: Usage
anyOf.description: Token usage and cost information for a task run.

usage.description: Usage information for the task run. This includes the number of input tokens, output tokens, and total tokens used.

anyOf.title: ChatCompletionAssistantMessageParamWrapper
anyOf.description:
Almost exact copy of ChatCompletionAssistantMessageParam, but two changes.

First change: List[T] instead of Iterable[T] for tool_calls. Addresses pydantic issue.
https://github.com/pydantic/pydantic/issues/9541

Second change: Add reasoning_content to the message. A LiteLLM property for reasoning data.

trace.title: Trace
trace.description: The trace of the task run in OpenAI format. This is the list of messages that were sent to/from the model.

parent_task_run_id.title: Parent Task Run Id
parent_task_run_id.description: The ID of the parent task run. This is the ID of the task run that contains this task run.

schema.title: TaskRun
schema.description:
Represents a single execution of a Task.

Contains the input used, its source, the output produced, and optional
repair information if the output needed correction.

project_id.description: The unique identifier of the project.
project_id.title: Project Id

task_id.description: The unique identifier of the task within the project.
task_id.title: Task Id
